questions.q: What is the enterprise pattern stack and what pressure does each of the five patterns own?
questions.a: Service Layer (orchestration), Domain Model (invariants and business rules), Data Mapper (object-relational isolation), Repository (query abstraction), Unit of Work (transaction-scope coordination). Each owns one pressure; none overlap.

questions.q: How do Command, Observer, and State compose without overlapping responsibilities in an editor?
questions.a: Command owns action lifecycle (execute/undo/redo). Observer owns notification fan-out after a change. State owns current mode behavior. Each fires in sequence — Command executes, Observer notifies, State governs what behavior is valid — with no pattern owning another's concern.

questions.q: What is accidental complexity, and what test detects it in a pattern-heavy codebase?
questions.a: Accidental complexity is difficulty introduced by the design itself rather than the problem it solves. The test: attempt to remove the pattern and check if any behavior breaks or any present cost reappears. If removal changes nothing, the pattern was accidental structure.

questions.q: State the composition heuristic for adding patterns to a growing codebase.
questions.a: Add one pattern per one concrete pressure — only when that pressure actually appears in the code. Do not plan the full stack upfront. Each new pattern should own a distinct force that no existing pattern already addresses.
